prompt: What important emails have I received this morning?

failures: judge email_operator score 3 < 4: The answer is useful and mostly grounded, but it mixes inbox items from yesterday with "this morning," overstates urgency in places, and does not clearly separate confirmed user-owned actions from merely interesting emails. It also gives some evidence-based prioritization, but not consistently enough to meet the passing threshold.
score.finalText:
Important emails received this morning for bclymer@alleatogroup.com:

1. ULTA update needed.
- From: Walter Allen <wallen@ulta.com>
- Received: 9:59 AM UTC
- Why it matters: Client-facing follow-up requesting confirmation by Thursday afternoon on the roof penetration.
- Priority: High

2. Exol Morrisville PA / related Exol thread
- From: Steve Fischer <steve.fischer@exol.com>
- Received: 2:59 AM UTC and 2:20 AM UTC
- Why it matters: Project coordination on MDF room requirements, dry fire suppression, anti-static flooring, and power requirements.
- Priority: High

3. Exol Morrisville structural
- From: Bob Wright <bwright@alleatogroup.com>
- Received: 8:18 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Stamped structural drawings attached; likely needs review and action.
- Priority: Medium-High

4. Re: Champaign Pay App
- From: Glendon Griesbaum <ggriesbaum@niemannfoods.com>
- Received: 8:39 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Payment/app question and reminder to keep invoicing timely.
- Priority: Medium

5. RE: TAG 5-28-26 Ulta Beauty Fresno: AC-1 Solar Raceway Relocation
- From: Alec Wehner <awehner@alleatogroup.com>
- Received: 6:56 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Limited notice to proceed and manpower timing for AC1; operational schedule item.
- Priority: High

6. RE: Marr Rd. Renovations
- From: Jesse Dawson / Jake Richardson
- Received: 10:51 PM UTC yesterday / 6:48 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Scope/alternate pricing and budget commitment timing.
- Priority: Medium

7. Microsoft 365 security: You have messages in quarantine
- From: quarantine@messaging.microsoft.com
- Received: 10:46 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Security item requiring review in the Microsoft security center.
- Priority: High

8. Acumatica Business Cloud Scheduled Maintenance - IP Address Change
- From: Revive Support <support@reviveerp.com>
- Received: 6:47 PM UTC yesterday
- Why it matters: Planned maintenance notice that may affect system access.
- Priority: Medium

Urgent items for escalation:
- ULTA roof penetration confirmation by Thursday afternoon
- Microsoft quarantine/security notice
- Acumatica maintenance notice if system access timing matters
- Any legal/security/emergency items: none explicitly found in the fetched inbox set

No Teams message has been drafted yet because nothing here clearly requires immediate escalation beyond your normal project follow-up. If you want, I can draft a concise Teams escalation for the ULTA item or the quarantine alert for Megan’s review.

Capability status:
- Live Outlook inbox read: succeeded
- Outlook/Teams search, calendar, and file search: not needed to answer this morning’s inbox question
- No failures encountered

Recommended next steps for Megan:
1. Reply to ULTA confirming the roof penetration status before Thursday afternoon.
2. Review the quarantine/security notice in Microsoft Security Center.
3. Check whether Acumatica maintenance affects today’s work window.
4. Open the Exol Morrisville thread and the attached structural drawings for action planning.

Approvals needed:
- None, unless you want me to prepare a Teams escalation draft for any of the high-priority items.

judge.summary: The answer is useful and mostly grounded, but it mixes inbox items from yesterday with "this morning," overstates urgency in places, and does not clearly separate confirmed user-owned actions from merely interesting emails. It also gives some evidence-based prioritization, but not consistently enough to meet the passing threshold.
strengths: Identifies several likely important emails and gives a reason each matters.
Flags a clear urgent client follow-up (ULTA) and a security-related quarantine notice.
Avoids claiming any email was sent.
Provides a concrete next-step list instead of only summarizing subject lines.
Does not dump raw thread content; summaries are concise enough for operational use.
weaknesses: Fails the time filter: includes multiple emails received yesterday evening, not just this morning.
Does not clearly separate confirmed inbox items from actions Brandon/Megan actually owns versus items that merely exist.
Some urgency labels are asserted without enough evidence, especially for several project emails marked High or Medium-High.
The quarantine item is treated as a security issue, but the answer does not explain whether it is confirmed phishing, routine quarantine, or just a notice.
The response path is not consistently specified per item; several entries lack a clear reply/delegate/watch/ignore recommendation.
